subject: Project update
snippet: Hey Nate, just checking in on where things stand with the QI project. Last we sp...
body:
Hey Nate, just checking in on where things stand with the QI project. Last we spoke you were still waiting on the data pull from informatics — any movement there?

We have the department meeting next week and I'd like to have something concrete to share. Even a rough timeline would help. Let me know what you've got.

subject: Re: Project update
snippet: Hi Phil, good timing — I heard back from informatics yesterday. They can have th...
body:
Hi Phil, good timing — I heard back from informatics yesterday. They can have the de-identified dataset ready by end of next week, which puts us roughly on schedule.

I'm planning to have a preliminary analysis ready before the department meeting. I'll send you a draft slide or two so you can review before you present. Does that work?

Thanks for keeping this on my radar.

subject: Team meeting Thursday
snippet: Hi Nate, I'm putting together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting and wanted ...
body:
Hi Nate, I'm putting together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting and wanted your input before I circulate it. So far I have: QI project update, Q3 scheduling changes, and a brief overview of the new Epic module rollout.

Anything you think we should add or push to a future meeting? I want to keep it under an hour if possible. Let me know by tomorrow so I can send it out Wednesday.

subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday
snippet: Phil, the agenda looks solid. I'd suggest adding a quick item on the new on-call...
body:
Phil, the agenda looks solid. I'd suggest adding a quick item on the new on-call coverage rotation — there's been some confusion about assignments and it'd be better to sort it out in person than over email.

Also, would you be open to leaving 10 minutes at the end for open Q&A? Last meeting people had things they wanted to raise but we ran out of time. Happy to defer to your call on that.

subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday
snippet: Good call on both. I'll slot the coverage rotation in after the Epic update and ...
body:
Good call on both. I'll slot the coverage rotation in after the Epic update and build in 10 minutes at the end for open items.

I'll send the final agenda to the team tomorrow morning. Thanks for the quick turnaround, Nate — see you Thursday.

subject: Manager 1:1 prep
snippet: Hey, your 1:1 with Dr. Keller is Thursday, right? Have you thought about what yo...
body:
Hey, your 1:1 with Dr. Keller is Thursday, right? Have you thought about what you want to bring up? Last time you mentioned wanting to start the conversation about the path to associate director and then never did.

Just don't want you to go in without a plan and spend the whole 30 minutes on logistics. You always say you wish you'd prepared more after the fact.

subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep
snippet: You're right, I keep putting it off. Yes it's Thursday at 2. I do want to raise ...
body:
You're right, I keep putting it off. Yes it's Thursday at 2. I do want to raise the career path thing — I've been holding back but I think it's time.

I'm also thinking about bringing up workload distribution on the team. It's been uneven and I don't think Keller fully sees it. Any advice on how to frame that without it sounding like a complaint?

subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep
snippet: For the workload issue, frame it around sustainability and patient care, not fai...
body:
For the workload issue, frame it around sustainability and patient care, not fairness. Something like 'I want to flag a capacity concern before it affects outcomes.' That makes it a professional observation, not a grievance.

For the career path conversation — just be direct. Ask what the criteria are and what she'd need to see from you over the next year. You've been there long enough that it won't come across as overstepping.

You've got this.

subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep
snippet: That framing is really helpful, thank you. I'll lead with the capacity issue and...
body:
That framing is really helpful, thank you. I'll lead with the capacity issue and then pivot to the career conversation. I might write a few bullet points tonight so I don't freeze up in the moment.

I'll let you know how it goes. Thanks for always pushing me to actually prepare for these things.

subject: Training reminder
snippet: Hey, did you see the HR email about mandatory compliance training? The deadline ...
body:
Hey, did you see the HR email about mandatory compliance training? The deadline is this Friday and I know how these things sneak up on you. It's the annual HIPAA refresher plus a new section on workplace conduct.

It's probably 45 minutes total. Can you knock it out tonight or tomorrow?

subject: Re: Training reminder
snippet: Ugh, I saw it and then completely forgot. Thanks for the nudge. I'll try to star...
body:
Ugh, I saw it and then completely forgot. Thanks for the nudge. I'll try to start it tonight after the kids are in bed.

Do you know if it saves progress? Last year I had to sit through the whole thing in one shot and it was rough.

subject: Re: Training reminder
snippet: Mine saved progress when I did it last week, so you should be fine. Log in throu...
body:
Mine saved progress when I did it last week, so you should be fine. Log in through the HR portal — it's the HealthStream link. Your usual credentials should work.

Please don't wait until Friday morning. You always do that and then it becomes a whole stressful thing.

subject: Re: Training reminder
snippet: I started it. First two modules are done. It does save progress so I'll finish t...
body:
I started it. First two modules are done. It does save progress so I'll finish the rest tomorrow at lunch.

And for the record, I was not going to wait until Friday morning. Probably.

subject: Re: Training reminder
snippet: Ha. I'll believe it when I see the completion email. Good job starting it though...
body:
Ha. I'll believe it when I see the completion email. Good job starting it though, seriously.

Just a heads up — the last module is the new conduct policy one and it has a short quiz at the end. Nothing hard, just actually read it before you click through.

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: Hey man, it's been a minute. Any chance you're free for lunch this week? I'm thi...
body:
Hey man, it's been a minute. Any chance you're free for lunch this week? I'm thinking Thursday or Friday — there's a new taco spot on Meridian that's been getting great reviews and I've been wanting to check it out.

Let me know if either day works for you.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday is perfect, let's do it. I've walked by that place twice now and kept m...
body:
Thursday is perfect, let's do it. I've walked by that place twice now and kept meaning to try it. Noon-ish work for you?

Looking forward to it — it really has been too long.

subject: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Hey Nate, any chance you're free Tuesday morning? I'll be working remotely from ...
body:
Hey Nate, any chance you're free Tuesday morning? I'll be working remotely from the city and thought we could grab coffee before my first call. Maybe 8 or 8:30 at Elliot Bay?

No pressure if you've got a packed morning, just figured it was worth asking.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: 8:30 works great — I can do an hour before I need to be in. Elliot Bay is perfec...
body:
8:30 works great — I can do an hour before I need to be in. Elliot Bay is perfect, I haven't been there in ages.

See you Tuesday. I'll grab us a table.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Perfect. I'll aim for 8:30 sharp. If I'm a few minutes late, go ahead and order ...
body:
Perfect. I'll aim for 8:30 sharp. If I'm a few minutes late, go ahead and order me a cortado — you know the one.

Looking forward to it. Got some stuff I want to catch up with you on.

subject: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Nate! Marcus's 40th is coming up on the 19th and a few of us are putting togethe...
body:
Nate! Marcus's 40th is coming up on the 19th and a few of us are putting together a surprise brunch. We're thinking Sunday the 20th around 11am at Toulouse Petit.

Are you and Nicole free? And more importantly — can you keep a secret for three weeks? I know that's historically a challenge for you.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Ha, very funny. Yes we're free and yes I can keep a secret. Nicole absolutely ca...
body:
Ha, very funny. Yes we're free and yes I can keep a secret. Nicole absolutely can — she kept our own surprise party from me for two months so she's the expert here.

Toulouse Petit is a great pick, Marcus will love it. How many people are you expecting? And should we chip in on a group gift or are people doing their own thing?

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: About 12 confirmed so far. Keisha is organizing a group gift — she's Venmo-reque...
body:
About 12 confirmed so far. Keisha is organizing a group gift — she's Venmo-requesting everyone $30 toward a weekend experience thing, which is very Marcus.

I'm working on getting a private room at the restaurant and will send a calendar invite once it's locked. Can you reach out to Dave and Priya? I've lost track of their contact info and you were better friends with them from the camping trip.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: I'll text Dave tonight and I think I still have Priya's number from last summer ...
body:
I'll text Dave tonight and I think I still have Priya's number from last summer — I'll reach out to both and report back by end of week.

This is going to be great. Marcus has no idea, I assume? Send the calendar invite whenever you're ready. We're locked in.

subject: Renewal options
snippet: Hey, our Spotify family plan is up for renewal next month and I got an email say...
body:
Hey, our Spotify family plan is up for renewal next month and I got an email saying the price is going up to $17.99. I also noticed YouTube Music has a family plan for $14.99 that includes YouTube Premium.

Worth switching or is the hassle not worth it? You use it more than I do so I wanted your take before I did anything.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: The YouTube Music bundle is tempting honestly, mostly for the YouTube Premium — ...
body:
The YouTube Music bundle is tempting honestly, mostly for the YouTube Premium — I watch enough YouTube that we'd probably save money overall when you factor that in.

My only hesitation is migrating my Spotify playlists, which are kind of a mess. How many people are on the family plan right now — is it just us and the kids or did we add anyone else?

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: It's just us four — you, me, Lily, and Noah. My dad was on it for a while but I ...
body:
It's just us four — you, me, Lily, and Noah. My dad was on it for a while but I removed him when he admitted he never actually used it.

I looked into the migration issue and there's a free tool called Soundiiz that can transfer playlists between services. Might be worth trying before we commit. Want me to look into it more and do a test run?

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Yeah, go ahead and test Soundiiz. If it moves the main playlists over cleanly I'...
body:
Yeah, go ahead and test Soundiiz. If it moves the main playlists over cleanly I'm on board with switching. The $3 monthly savings alone is fine, but YouTube Premium on top makes it a no-brainer.

Just don't cancel Spotify until we've confirmed the transfer worked — the last thing I want is to lose the kids' playlists and have to rebuild them from scratch.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Agreed — I'll run a test transfer this weekend and keep Spotify active until we'...
body:
Agreed — I'll run a test transfer this weekend and keep Spotify active until we're sure everything is intact.

If it works, I'll switch us over before the renewal hits on the 15th so we don't get charged again. I'll let you know how the test goes Sunday.

subject: Vendor invoice question
snippet: Hey, I got an invoice from Greenfield Landscaping for $340 but I thought we agre...
body:
Hey, I got an invoice from Greenfield Landscaping for $340 but I thought we agreed on $280 for the spring cleanup. I can't find the original quote email quickly.

Do you remember what the final number was? Did something change when they came out?

subject: Re: Vendor invoice question
snippet: The original quote was $280, but when they were here I asked them to also trim t...
body:
The original quote was $280, but when they were here I asked them to also trim the hedges along the back fence while they had the equipment out — I forgot to mention that to you. That's almost certainly the $60 difference.

The extra charge is fair given the additional work, so go ahead and pay it. Remind me next time to get any add-ons in writing beforehand so we're not caught off guard.

subject: Apartment lease renewal
snippet: Hey, the renewal packet from Greystone arrived today. They're offering a 12-mont...
body:
Hey, the renewal packet from Greystone arrived today. They're offering a 12-month renewal at $2,350 (up from $2,200) or month-to-month at $2,600. Deadline to respond is July 15th.

We should talk about this soon. Are you still thinking we might want to buy something in the next 12 to 18 months? That changes things a lot.

subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal
snippet: Yeah, the timing is tricky. I don't want to lock in 12 months if we're serious a...
body:
Yeah, the timing is tricky. I don't want to lock in 12 months if we're serious about buying, but $2,600 a month for month-to-month is a lot of extra cash burning while we're trying to save for a down payment.

My gut says take the 12-month at $2,350 and use the year to get serious — get pre-approved, find an agent, actually look at neighborhoods. But I want to hear your thinking. Can we sit down this weekend and go through the numbers?

subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal
snippet: I think you're right. Month-to-month only makes sense if we're close to making a...
body:
I think you're right. Month-to-month only makes sense if we're close to making an offer on something, and we're not there yet.

Let's do the 12-month and treat it as a hard deadline to get our act together on the buying side. I'll respond to Greystone before the 15th. Saturday evening work to talk through the timeline?

subject: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: Sharing highlights from this week's newsletters since you asked me to start forw...
body:
Sharing highlights from this week's newsletters since you asked me to start forwarding the good ones. From The Dispatch: a really sharp piece on healthcare financing that I think is relevant to what's going on at Harborview. From Stratechery: Ben Thompson's latest on AI and hospital administration — it's long but worth the time.

Also one from Kara Swisher's newsletter that's more of a fun read. Links below.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: The Stratechery piece is already in my read-later queue — glad you flagged it, I...
body:
The Stratechery piece is already in my read-later queue — glad you flagged it, I'll bump it up. The healthcare financing one sounds like exactly what I should read before my next meeting with administration.

Haven't heard of the Swisher newsletter — is that new? Also, can you add me directly to whatever list you're getting The Dispatch from? I keep forgetting to subscribe myself.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: The Swisher one is called 'On with Kara Swisher' — it's been around a while but ...
body:
The Swisher one is called 'On with Kara Swisher' — it's been around a while but I only subscribed a few months ago. Mostly tech and media commentary but she's sharp and it's a quick read.

For The Dispatch, just go to thedispatch.com — the free tier has most of the good stuff. I'll text you the link right now so it doesn't get lost.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: Just subscribed to both — free tier on The Dispatch for now to see if it sticks....
body:
Just subscribed to both — free tier on The Dispatch for now to see if it sticks. Adding Kara Swisher too since you vouched for it.

Keep forwarding highlights even after I'm subscribed though. You have better editorial judgment about what I'll actually read versus what I'll just let pile up. Appreciate this.

subject: Schedule check — kids pickup
snippet: Hey, can you check your schedule and confirm your pickup days this week? I have ...
body:
Hey, can you check your schedule and confirm your pickup days this week? I have a dentist appointment Wednesday at 4:30 and a work thing Thursday evening, so I need you to cover both those days.

Also, Lily has soccer practice Friday at 5 — are you okay to take her or do you need me to find a carpool?

subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup
snippet: Wednesday and Thursday are no problem, I'll handle pickup both days. I have a la...
body:
Wednesday and Thursday are no problem, I'll handle pickup both days. I have a late call Thursday but I can move it earlier — I'll check with the team today.

For Lily's soccer Friday, I can do it, but if the carpool with the Hendersons is happening that would actually be easier. Do you know if they're going that week?

subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup
snippet: I'll text Jen Henderson and check. I think they usually go to Friday practice bu...
body:
I'll text Jen Henderson and check. I think they usually go to Friday practice but I haven't confirmed this week.

Also — don't forget Noah has early dismissal Wednesday at 2:30, not the usual 3:15. I'll put a note on the fridge but wanted to flag it here too so it's actually on your radar.

subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup
snippet: Good catch on the early dismissal — I absolutely would have shown up at 3:15. 2:...
body:
Good catch on the early dismissal — I absolutely would have shown up at 3:15. 2:30 Wednesday, got it. Putting it in my calendar right now.

Let me know what Jen says about Friday. If they can't do the carpool I'll take Lily myself, it's not a big deal either way.

subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup
snippet: Jen confirmed — they can take Lily Friday and will drop her home after practice....
body:
Jen confirmed — they can take Lily Friday and will drop her home after practice. So you're just on the hook for Wednesday and Thursday.

This is exactly why we need to actually use the shared calendar instead of sorting this out over email every single week. I'll set up a recurring schedule this weekend if you remind me.

subject: Family vacation planning
snippet: Hey, I've been looking at August options and narrowed it down to two: (1) a beac...
body:
Hey, I've been looking at August options and narrowed it down to two: (1) a beach house rental in Ocean Shores for a full week, or (2) a long weekend in Victoria, BC — ferry over, stay at the Empress, the whole thing.

The kids would probably vote for the beach, but I feel like Victoria would be more of a real experience. What's your gut?

subject: Re: Family vacation planning
snippet: My gut says Victoria. The beach house is fun but we've done it twice already and...
body:
My gut says Victoria. The beach house is fun but we've done it twice already and the novelty has worn off a bit. The ferry ride alone would be a genuine adventure for the kids, and Victoria is beautiful — we haven't been since before Lily was born.

Can you send me a cost breakdown for both when you get a chance? I want to make sure the Victoria logistics work with our budget before we commit, but I'm leaning that way.

subject: Weekend plans
snippet: Hey, what are we doing this weekend? I don't think we locked anything down. My p...
body:
Hey, what are we doing this weekend? I don't think we locked anything down. My parents mentioned possibly coming over Saturday evening for dinner — would that work for you?

Also, are you up for getting out with the kids Sunday? I was thinking farmers market in the morning and the park after.

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Saturday dinner with your parents sounds great — tell them to come around 6. I c...
body:
Saturday dinner with your parents sounds great — tell them to come around 6. I can grill if the weather cooperates, otherwise I'll make the pasta dish your mom likes.

Sunday farmers market is a yes from me. Let's aim for 9ish before it gets too crowded. The kids have been inside too much lately and the park after sounds like a good way to tire them out.

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Perfect, I'll let them know 6 on Saturday. My mom is going to want the pasta — s...
body:
Perfect, I'll let them know 6 on Saturday. My mom is going to want the pasta — she mentions that dish every time, so maybe grab the ingredients Friday so we're not scrambling.

I'll pack a bag for Sunday. This sounds like a really nice weekend honestly — we've been running around too much lately. Looking forward to it.
